client_information.occupation: Senior Project Manager (remote) in a healthcare software company
client_information.how_you_heard_and_why_success_strategy_s:
I first heard about your services through a friend from my local women-in-business networking group, Jenna Morales. She mentioned that she had worked with you about a year ago when she was feeling overwhelmed running her small marketing agency from home, and she said your systems and accountability made a huge difference in getting her office, email, and schedule under control. She forwarded me a link to one of your blog posts about "decision fatigue and clutter" and I ended up going down the rabbit hole on your website, reading several articles and watching one of your short videos. I also later realized I had heard your name mentioned on a productivity podcast (I think it was "The Organize 365" podcast), where you were interviewed about the connection between physical clutter and mental bandwidth.

Why I want a Success Strategy Session:
Over the past three years, my work situation and home life have both shifted pretty dramatically, and I feel like my systems never really caught up. I used to commute to an office and had very clear boundaries between work and home. Since the pandemic, I’ve been working fully remote as a senior project manager for a healthcare software company. At the same time, my husband and I bought our first house (a 3-bedroom place in San Diego), my mom downsized and passed a lot of her belongings to me, and we now have a very energetic 5-year-old son. In short, the volume of stuff in our home has exploded, my work responsibilities have increased, and my ability to stay on top of everything has not.

Concretely, my home office is a constant source of stress. I have piles of project documents, printouts, half-used notebooks, unopened mail, and random kid art all over my desk and the floor around it. I have a file cabinet that I never fully set up, so things just get stacked in front of it. I keep telling myself I’ll do a big weekend declutter, but weekends get eaten up by errands, birthday parties, and just needing to rest. I end up working from the dining table or the couch because my actual desk feels chaotic and embarrassing, which then causes more household clutter to overlap with work stuff.

On the digital side, my email inbox is a mess—both work and personal. I have thousands of unread messages and no real system for processing them. I use a digital calendar, but I’m not consistent about blocking time for focused work or for household tasks, so everything feels reactive. When I’m supposed to be focusing on a deep-work task, I’m distracted by the physical clutter on my desk or I suddenly remember I never paid a bill or responded to a school email. I’m constantly context-switching, which is draining and makes me feel like I’m doing lots of little things but not actually moving big projects forward.

Emotionally, I notice that I feel ashamed and frustrated when people drop by unexpectedly or when my son wants to have a friend over. I scramble to clear the dining table, shove piles into the guest room, and promise myself I’ll sort them later. I also notice that I sometimes buy duplicates of items (like tape, batteries, and even certain pantry items) because I can’t find what we already have. That makes me feel wasteful and disorganized, which clashes with how competent I feel in other areas of my life.

I’m seeking a Success Strategy Session specifically because I don’t just want a one-time "tidy up"—I want to understand the underlying patterns that keep me stuck in this cycle, and I want a realistic, sustainable system that fits my actual life and energy levels. I’d like help prioritizing where to start (home office vs. whole house vs. schedule/time management), and I’d appreciate an outside perspective to help me see what’s possible. I know from experience that I respond really well to structure and accountability when it’s tailored to me, and I feel like I’m too close to my own situation to design that structure alone.

My main goals are to:
1) Create a functional, calm home office where I can actually focus and feel professional.
2) Develop simple daily and weekly routines that prevent clutter from building up again (rather than relying on massive clean-up sessions).
3) Improve my time management so that I’m not always working late at night to compensate for a scattered day.
4) Feel more in control of my environment so that I can be more present with my family and less mentally preoccupied with "all the things I haven’t done."

I’m very willing to be honest about what has and hasn’t worked for me in the past, and I’m ready to make changes. I just need guidance, structure, and a plan that feels doable instead of overwhelming.
